title: Flow device and methods of creating different pressure drops based on a direction of flow
abstract: A flow device includes a flow-through region comprising at least one stage and configured to create a first pressure drop across the flow-through region in response to flow through the flow-through region in a first direction and a second pressure drop in response to flow through the flow-through region in a second direction, the first pressure drop being less than the second pressure drop under the same flow rates.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A flow device (10), comprising: a flow-through region (14) comprising at least one stage (18) and configured to create a first pressure drop across the flow-through region (14) in response to flow through the flow- through region in a first direction and a second pressure drop in response to flow through the flow-through region in a second direction, the first pressure drop being less than the second pressure drop under the same flow rates. 2. The flow device (10) of claim 1, wherein the at least one stage includes a pocket (30) having a larger cross sectional flow area than a first opening (34) and a second opening (38) fluidically connected to the pocket (30). 3. The flow device (10) of claim 2, wherein the first opening (34) and the second opening (38) serve as inlets and outlets to the pocket (30). 4. The flow device (10) of claim 2, wherein a baffle (120) is positioned nearer to the first opening (134) than the second opening (138). 5. The flow device (10) of claim 4, wherein the baffle (120) has a shape from the group consisting of "U" shaped, round, oval, polyhedral, zigzagged shaped, or combinations of two or more of the foregoing. 6. The flow device (10) of claim 4, wherein the baffle (120) splits the flow through the pocket (30) into multiple flows. 7. The flow device (10) of claim 4, wherein the flow device (10) is configured such that pressure drop through the pocket (30) is less in the first direction than in a second direction when flow in the first direction is defined as being into the pocket (30) through which ever of the first opening (34) and the second opening (38) is nearer to the baffle (120) and the second direction is opposite the first direction. 8. The flow device (10) of claim 2, wherein walls (46, 50) defining at least one of the first opening (34) and the second opening (38) are tapered relative to a direction of flow through the at least one of the first opening (34) and the second opening (38). 9. The flow device (10) of claim 8, wherein walls (46, 50) defining both the first opening (34) and the second opening (38) are tapered in a same direction relative to a direction of flow through both the first opening (34) and the second opening (38). 10. The flow device (10) of claim 2, wherein at least one of the first opening (334) and the second opening (338) has a wall (346) that is common with the pocket (330). 11. The flow device (10) of claim 2, wherein the first opening (34) is offset from the second opening (38). 12. The flow device (10) of claim 1, wherein the first pressure drop is in a range of about 40 to 60 percent of the second pressure drop with all other things being equal. 13. The flow device (10) of claim 1, wherein flow in the first direction is for treating an earth formation and flow in the second direction is for production from the earth formation. 14. The flow device (10) of claim 1, wherein a pad (420) is positioned nearer to the first opening (434) than the second opening (438) such that flow through the flow device (10) is altered more by the pad (420) when flow is into the flow device (10) through the second opening (438) than when flow is into the flow device (10) through the first opening (434). 15. The flow device (10) of claim 1, wherein, the flow device (10) includes no moving parts to create the difference in pressure drop between the first direction and the second direction. 16. The flow device (10) of claim 1, further comprising a baffle (520) positioned within the pocket (530) being "U" shaped with a concave side of the baffle (520) facing toward the second opening (538). 17. The flow device (10) of claim 16, wherein the baffle (520) is positioned equidistant between opposing walls (532) of the pocket (530) not including the first opening (534) or the second opening (538). 18. The flow device (10) of claim 16, wherein a first end (640) of the "U" shaped baffle (620) is nearer to a wall (644) of the pocket (630) through which the second opening (638) extends than a second end (648) of the "U" shaped baffle (620). 19. The flow device (10) of claim 1, wherein the flow device (10) is configured to create a different pressure drop to different fluids flowing therethrough. 20. The flow device (10) of claim 1, wherein pressure drop of oil flowing through the flow device (10) is less than that of water flowing through the flow device (10) all other things being equal. 21. A method of creating different pressure drops based on a direction of flow, comprising: flowing fluid at a set flow rate through a flow-through region (14) of a flow device (10) in a first direction through a first opening (34) into a pocket (30) and out of the pocket (30) through a second opening (38) and creating a first pressure drop in the process; and 

 flowing fluid at the set flow rate through the flow-through region (14) of the flow device (10) in a second direction through the second opening (38) into the pocket (30) and out of the pocket (30) through the first opening (34) and creating a second pressure drop in the process, the first pressure drop being less than the second pressure drop. 22. The method of creating different pressure drops based on a direction of flow of claim 21, further comprising impinging a baffle (120) nearer to the first opening (134) than to the second opening (138) with fluid entering the pocket (130). 23. The method of creating different pressure drops based on a direction of flow of claim 22, further comprising splitting fluid flowing through the first opening (134) into two flow paths with the baffle (120). 24. The method of creating different pressure drops based on a direction of flow of claim 22, further comprising impinging a flat surface (156) of the baffle (120) with fluid flowing through the first opening (134). 25. The method of creating different pressure drops based on a direction of flow of claim 22, further comprising impinging a curved (156) surface of the baffle (120) with fluid flowing through the first opening (134). 26. The method of creating different pressure drops based on a direction of flow of claim 22, further comprising impinging a concave surface (156) of the baffle (120) with fluid flowing through the first opening 1(34). 27. The method of creating different pressure drops based on a direction of flow of claim 22, further comprising impinging a convex surface (156) of the baffle (120) with fluid flowing through the first opening (134). 28. The method of creating different pressure drops based on a direction of flow of claim 21, further comprising altering flow in the second direction greater than in the first direction with a pad (420) positioned nearer to the first opening (434) than to the second opening (438). 29. The method of creating different pressure drops based on a direction of flow of claim 21, further comprising creating the first pressure drop that is less than the second pressure drop without moving parts within the first opening (34), the second opening (38) or the pocket (30) to create the difference in pressure drop. 30. A method of creating different pressure drops based on a direction of flow, comprising: 

 flowing fluid at a set flow rate through a flow-through region (14) of a flow device (10) in a first direction through a first opening (34) into a pocket (30) and out through a second opening (38); accelerating fluid as it flows through at least one of the first opening (34) and the second opening (38) with a decrease in a cross sectional flow area of the at least one first opening (34) and second opening (38) and creating a first pressure drop in the process; flowing fluid at the set flow rate through the flow-through region (14) of the flow device (10) in a second direction through the second opening (38) into the pocket (30) and out through the first opening (34); and decelerating fluid as it flows through at least one of the second opening (38) and the first opening (34) with an increase in a cross sectional flow area of the at least one second opening (38) and first opening and creating a second pressure drop in the process, the first pressure drop being less than the second pressure drop. 31. A method of creating different pressure drops based on a direction of flow, comprising: flowing fluid at a set flow rate through a flow-through region (14) of a flow device (10) in a first direction through a first opening (134) into a pocket (130) toward a convex side (156) of a baffle (120) and out of the pocket (130) through a second opening (138) and creating a first pressure drop in the process; and flowing fluid at the set flow rate through the flow-through region (14) of the flow device (10) in a second direction through the second opening (138) into the pocket (130) toward a concave side (156) of the baffle (120) and out of the pocket (130) through the first opening (134) and creating a second pressure drop in the process, the first pressure drop being less than the second pressure drop with no part moving within the first opening (134), the second opening (138) or the pocket (130) to create the difference in pressure drop. 32. The method of creating different pressure drops based on a direction of flow of claim 31, further comprising impinging a baffle (120) nearer to the second opening (138) than to the first opening (134) with fluid entering the pocket (130). 33. The method of creating different pressure drops based on a direction of flow of claim 31, further comprising splitting fluid flowing through the first opening into two flow paths with the baffle (120). 34. The method of creating different pressure drops based on a direction of flow of claim 31, further comprising impinging one end of the concave shaped baffle (120) 

 positioned nearer the second opening (138) with fluid flowing into the pocket (130) through the second opening (138) than the other end of the concave shaped baffle (120).
